copy.caption: The tallest animal alive — and it still has to stretch.
copy.shortDescription: A giraffe's neck is nearly two meters long, but getting a drink of water still means spreading those long legs wide and lowering all that height toward a puddle, which is one of the most awkward and beautiful things in nature. Their tongue is dark purplish-blue and long enough to wrap around a branch. Fun fact: Giraffes only sleep around thirty minutes a day — in short naps of a few minutes each, standing up.
